1.title: Security Policy
1.preview: # Security Policy To report a security vulnerability, please use the [Tidelift security contact](https://tidelift.com/security). Tidelift will coordinate the fix and disclosure.

3.title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
3.preview: ## Scope This Code of Conduct applies both within project spaces and in public spaces when an individual is representing the project or its community. Examples of representing a project or community include using an official project e-mail

4.title: Contributing to Chalk
4.preview: # Contributing to Chalk Please note that this project is released with a [Contributor Code of Conduct](code-of-conduct.md). By participating in this project you agree to abide by its terms.

6.title: readme.md
6.preview: ## Usage ```js import chalk from 'chalk'; console.log(chalk.blue('Hello world!')); ``` Chalk comes with an easy to use composable API where you just chain and nest the styles you want. ```js import chalk from 'chalk'; const log = conso

7.title: readme.md
7.preview: ### chalk.level Specifies the level of color support. Color support is automatically detected, but you can override it by setting the `level` property. You should however only do this in your own code as it applies globally to all Chalk c

8.title: readme.md
8.preview: ### Modifiers - `reset` - Reset the current style. - `bold` - Make the text bold. - `dim` - Make the text have lower opacity. - `italic` - Make the text italic. *(Not widely supported)* - `underline` - Put a horizontal line below the text.

9.title: readme.md
9.preview: ## 256 and Truecolor color support Chalk supports 256 colors and [Truecolor](https://github.com/termstandard/colors) (16 million colors) on supported terminal apps. Colors are downsampled from 16 million RGB values to an ANSI color format

10.title: readme.md
10.preview: ### Why not switch to a smaller coloring package? Chalk may be larger, but there is a reason for that. It offers a more user-friendly API, well-documented types, supports millions of colors, and covers edge cases that smaller alternatives
